;<span id="R-Metric">R-Metric</span> (Recall Metric)
:[[File:Recall_metric.jpg|thumb|SuperMemo: Algorithm SM-19 performance metric]]Absolute measure of [[Glossary:Spaced repetition|spaced repetition]] algorithm performance based on their ability to predict recall before a [[Glossary:Grade|grade]] is scored. As of [https://supermemo.store/products/supermemo-20-for-windows SuperMemo 20], '''R-Metric''' compares [https://supermemo.guru/wiki/Algorithm_SM-19 Algorithm SM-19] against an optimally weighted average of predictions from SM-2, [https://supermemo.guru/wiki/Algorithm_SM-15 SM-15], [https://supermemo.guru/wiki/Algorithm_SM-19 SM-19], [https://supermemo.guru/wiki/Algorithm_SM-20 SM-20], and [[FSRS]]. The weighting is determined by [[Algorithm Arena]], where more accurate algorithms gain weight. '''R-Metric''' is shown as a percentage in '''[[Toolkit menu|Toolkit]] : [[Toolkit menu#Statistics|Statistics]] : [[Analysis]] : [[Analysis#Use|Use]] : Efficiency : [[Analysis#Use_:Efficiency:_R-Metric|R-Metric]]'''. It is the difference between the performance of the two competing predictions: <code>R-Metric=LSRM(Alg-19)-LSRM(Arena)</code>, where <code>LSRM</code> is the least squares predicted recall measure. '''R-Metric''' less than zero shows the superiority of [https://supermemo.guru/wiki/Algorithm_SM-19 Algorithm SM-19]. '''R-Metric''' greater than zero indicates the superiority of the weighted algorithm blend. <code>LSRM</code> is the square root of the average of squared absolute differences in recall predictions: <code>abs(Recall-PredictedRecall)</code>, where <code>Recall</code> is 0 for failing [[Glossary:Grade|grades]] and 1 for passing [[Glossary:Grade|grades]]. <code>PredictedRecall</code> is the prediction issued by the algorithm before the [[Glossary:Repetition|repetition]]. Expect a negative metric before [[Algorithm Arena]] determines how well individual algorithms perform in the collection.
